headline: Raymond James Recruiting Spend Helps Drive Net New Asset Gains
summary: Raymond James also said the majority of its $1.1 billion annual technology spend was going toward its private client business, including the rollout of AI agent Rai.

headline: Raymond James upgrades On Holding, downgrades Deckers ahead of earnings
summary: Investing.com -- Raymond James has reshuffled its sportswear and softlines ratings ahead of first-quarter earnings, lifting On Holding to Strong Buy while stepping back on Deckers, as the firm navigates a consumer backdrop shaped by tax refund tailwinds and rising oil prices.

headline: Raymond James Q2 Earnings Beat on Higher Revenues, Cost Woes Remain
summary: RJF tops Q2 earnings estimates on record revenues and asset growth, but rising expenses temper gains as costs climb across most components.

headline: RJF Q1 Deep Dive: Adviser Recruiting and Technology Investment Drive Growth Amid Stable Margins
summary: Financial services firm Raymond James Financial (NYSE:RJF) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 25.2% year on year to $4.26 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.83 per share was 2.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Raymond James CEO: Market sentiment among investors 'still high historically' despite war
